reasoning: France are the clear market and model favorites after a perfect group stage and a dominant 3-0 round-of-32 win over Sweden, with their outright price shortening to around +180 and their reach-the-final odds dipping under even money for the first time; scoring three-plus goals in nearly every match, Les Bleus have both firepower and depth. Argentina, the defending champions, drew the softer side of the bracket after winning Group J 3-0-0 and are the next shortest at roughly +400-425, setting up a potential 2022 final rematch that could only occur in the quarterfinals. Spain remain the highest-ceiling third option with Yamal and Pedri but have looked labored (a 0-0 with Cape Verde), while England, Brazil and Portugal round out the top tier without yet convincing.
